_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
alos: Additional 2-3 days based on the clinical condition and primary disease
clinical_indications: Torrential hemorrhage or increasing rate of bleeding, tamponade

preauth.label: Clinical notes including previous surgery details, evaluation findings, and planned line of management

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes – all vitals, history especially previous surgery details when performed, symptoms, signs, physical examination, indication for procedure, and planned line of treatment?
Was the clinical condition, previous surgery details, and imaging confirming the diagnosis?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge provided?

common_queries: Clinical condition, previous surgery details, or imaging not indicative of surgery for re-exploration

Clinical notes, previous surgery details, evaluation findings, or planned line of management missing or incomplete at pre-authorization (or submitted later without adequate justification for emergency)
Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with daily vitals and treatment details missing or incomplete at claim submission
Detailed Procedure / Operative notes missing or incomplete at claim submission
Blood transfusion notes (if applicable) missing at claim submission
Discharge summary with follow-up advice missing or incomplete at claim submission
Lack of documentation for factors indicating re-exploration (e.g., torrential hemorrhage, hemodynamic instability, large hemothorax, minimal coagulopathy)

clinical_key_pointers: Post-operative bleeding requiring surgical re-exploration is a frequent complication following cardiac surgery. Re-exploration for bleeding is associated with increased operative mortality and morbidity after cardiac surgery.
The interplay between time to re-exploration for bleeding and volume bled before must be taken in the context of the patient`s clinical picture and no one factor should be relied upon.
The re-sternotomy rate for bleeding or tamponade following first pericardiotomy is ~1-2%.
Resternotomy is best performed in OR (sterility, light, equipment), but if patient too unstable to transfer can be performed at bedside in ICU.
